title: Trump Reads Iran's Secret 14-Point Plan to End the War
summary: Imagine getting a secret note with 14 ideas to end a big fight. That's what just landed on President Trump's desk! On Saturday, he said he was reading a new proposal from Iran to stop the war between them. "I'll let you know about it later," he told reporters before hopping on Air Force One. Two Iranian news groups say the 14-point plan was sent through Pakistan, answering an earlier 9-point U.S. plan. Trump had said no to a different Iranian idea just days ago, but talks are still happening. For three weeks, a ceasefire (when both sides agree to stop fighting for a while) has mostly held. That's good news, because a lot of the world's oil travels through a narrow waterway called the Strait of Hormuz, and the fighting had blocked it. The U.S. even warned ships not to pay Iran for safe passage, or they could get in trouble. Meanwhile, a brave woman named Narges Mohammadi is in danger. She won the Nobel Peace Prize for standing up for people's rights in Iran, but she's in prison and very sick. On Friday, she fainted and was rushed to a hospital. Her family says she was beaten when she was arrested in December, and now her heart is weak. Her husband says Iran's secret police won't let her go to her own doctors in the capital. "Her body does not have the readiness," he said, worried she might die. The group that gives the Nobel Prize begged Iran to move her to safety. Also, Iran said it hanged two men it says were spies for Israel. Rights groups say Iran often holds secret trials where people can't defend themselves.
why_it_matters: When big fights block oil shipments, it can make gasoline prices rise, which might mean your family pays more for car trips and heating.

keywords.explanation: An agreement where both sides promise to stop fighting for a while.
A formal plan or suggestion offered for discussion.
A narrow waterway where a huge amount of the world's oil is shipped through.
Suddenly lost consciousness and passed out.
Being in a state of being prepared or strong enough.
Killed by tying a rope around the neck and dropping the person.

questions.question: Which country sent the 14-point peace plan?
What is the Strait of Hormuz important for?
Why is Narges Mohammadi in danger?
options: She is very sick in prison
She is trapped in a burning building
She is stuck in a traffic jam
questions.correct_answer: She is very sick in prison

questions.question: What did Iran say about the two men it hanged?
options: They were helping the Nobel group

questions.question: What did the U.S. warn ships not to do?
What did the Nobel Prize group ask Iran to do?

background_read: President Donald Trump (the U.S. president since 2025) has had tense relations with Iran over its nuclear program and military actions.
The Nobel Peace Prize is awarded in Norway each year to people who work bravely for peace and human rights.
The Strait of Hormuz is one of the most important waterways in the world because about one-fifth of all oil passes through it daily.
Article_Structure: WHO: President Trump, Iran’s government, and Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi
WHAT: Iran sent a new peace plan while a ceasefire holds, but a brave woman is dangerously sick in prison and two men were executed.
WHERE: The secret messages move between the U.S. and Iran, the fighting blocks the Strait of Hormuz, and Narges Mohammadi is held in Iran’s capital.
WHY: The oil blockage is hurting the world, and Iran is accused by many of mistreating prisoners and holding unfair trials.

perspectives.description: They want to negotiate an end to the war and get rid of harsh economic penalties that hurt their people.
It does not fully trust Iran’s promises and wants to protect oil routes and prevent threats to the region.
They are deeply worried that a Nobel Peace Prize winner is very sick in prison and demand she get proper medical care immediately.
